reason: NCT03762850 is real PROTECT (sparsentan vs irbesartan, Heerspink 2023 Lancet DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)00569-X) but tE/tN/cE/cN appear mismatched to the published 280-day eGFR endpoint; estimandType RR with HR=0.58 for proteinuria is not how PROTECT is reported.
ground_truth_hint: PROTECT primary endpoint is 36-week UPCR reduction; PMID 36966783 / DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)00569-X

reason: APPLAUSE-IgAN/iptacopan is real (Perkovic NEJM 2025) but estimandType RR with HR=0.63 likely confused with proteinuria geometric mean ratio in the primary endpoint.
Name 'C017' is a sponsor study code used as trial name; same NCT (NCT01866111) is also assigned a different name ('YKP3089-C013') in id 2129 EPILEPSY_NEW_AEDS_REVIEW — internal contradiction.
ground_truth_hint: NCT01866111 is the YKP3089-C013/Krauss 2020 Lancet Neurol cenobamate Phase 3; DOI 10.1016/S1474-4422(19)30399-0

reason: Name 'C021' is a sponsor study code, not a published trial acronym; cenobamate C021 (study YKP3089-C021) was a long-term open-label safety study, NOT a controlled efficacy trial as the tE/cE counts and RR=3.16 imply.
ground_truth_hint: NCT02535091 / YKP3089-C021 is open-label safety extension (Sperling 2020 Epilepsia); not a randomized RR comparator

reason: Name 'BRIVA-1' is not a published brivaracetam trial acronym; real UCB Phase 3 brivaracetam trials are coded N01252/N01253/N01254/N01358. PubMed search 'BRIVA brivaracetam' returns zero. NCT01561508 does not match a known brivaracetam pivotal trial era.
ground_truth_hint: Real brivaracetam Phase 3 pivotal trials: N01252 (NCT01261324), N01253 (NCT00490035), N01358 (NCT01405508)

reason: Name 'BRIVA-2' is fabricated acronym; AND the same NCT (NCT01261325) is assigned name 'N01252' in id 2134 of EPILEPSY_NEW_AEDS_REVIEW — same trial labeled with two different names in the same dataset.
ground_truth_hint: NCT01261325 corresponds to a UCB brivaracetam Phase 3; the published code is in the N0125x series, not 'BRIVA-2'

reason: Name 'PER-304' is a perampanel sponsor code; real Phase 3 perampanel pivotals are Study 304/305/306 (French/Krauss). NCT01191957 era is plausible for a Phase 3 but the name as 'PER-304' doesn't match published Study 304 (NCT00699582).
ground_truth_hint: Perampanel Study 304 = NCT00699582 (French 2012 Neurology). NCT01191957 may be a different perampanel study.

reason: Name 'PER-305' is a sponsor code masquerading as trial name; Study 305 (real perampanel pivotal) = NCT00699972 (French 2012), not NCT01393743.
ground_truth_hint: Study 305 = NCT00699972

reason: NCT02091375 is GWPCARE1 (cannabidiol Dravet — Devinsky 2017 NEJM), NOT a fenfluramine trial. The same NCT appears as 'GWPCARE1' in id 2085 CBD_SEIZURE_REVIEW. Real fenfluramine Dravet Study 1 = NCT02682927 (Lagae 2019 Lancet).
ground_truth_hint: Fenfluramine Dravet Study 1 = NCT02682927 / PMID 31839279 / DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(19)32500-0

reason: Name 'Study-2' is a generic placeholder. Fenfluramine Dravet Study 2 published as Nabbout 2020 JAMA Neurol; need NCT verification.
ground_truth_hint: Fenfluramine Dravet Study 2 = NCT02926898 / PMID 31790543 / DOI 10.1001/jamaneurol.2019.4113

reason: PubMed search for 'POLARIS omalizumab chronic spontaneous urticaria Asia' returns zero hits. No published Asian POLARIS omalizumab-CSU pivotal exists with the cited 2018 publication year and these denominators.
ground_truth_hint: Real Asian omalizumab-CSU registry studies (Hide 2017 Allergol Int) used different acronyms; no POLARIS-Asia in PubMed

reason: Name 'POLARIS-Pediatric' returns zero PubMed hits with omalizumab pediatric CSU 12-17y. The real pediatric omalizumab CSU trial is unpublished/single arm or referenced differently.
ground_truth_hint: No POLARIS-Pediatric urticaria trial indexed in PubMed

reason: Name 'X-MAGIC' with omalizumab/MENA returns zero PubMed hits. No such MENA-region omalizumab CSU trial exists in literature.
ground_truth_hint: No PubMed match for X-MAGIC urticaria omalizumab

reason: Per PubMed PMID 38008109 (Maurer 2024 Lancet, DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01684-7), PEARL-1 = NCT03580369 and PEARL-2 = NCT03580356 — the input swaps them. Also the HR=1.26 framing is wrong: PEARL was active-controlled vs omalizumab and ligelizumab was NOT superior to omalizumab.
ground_truth_hint: PEARL-1 = NCT03580369 (not NCT03580356)

reason: Per PubMed PMID 38008109 (Maurer 2024 Lancet, DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)01684-7), PEARL-2 = NCT03580356 and PEARL-1 = NCT03580369 — input has them swapped, mirror image of id 2103.
ground_truth_hint: PEARL-2 = NCT03580356 (not NCT03580369)

reason: Name 'EVOLVE-CSU' with omalizumab biosimilar 300mg returns zero specific PubMed hits; no published omalizumab biosimilar Phase 3 CSU pivotal exists with this acronym.
ground_truth_hint: No EVOLVE-CSU trial in PubMed indexed literature

reason: Name 'CUPID-A' for dupilumab CSU is not a known trial acronym. Real dupilumab CSU pivotals are LIBERTY-CUPID Study A/B (Maurer 2024 JACI). The CUPID-A label is plausible-sounding but the published trials use 'LIBERTY-CUPID' prefix and different NCTs.
ground_truth_hint: LIBERTY-CUPID Study A = NCT04180488 (Maurer 2024 J Allergy Clin Immunol)

reason: NCT04180488 is LIBERTY-CUPID Study A (dupilumab CSU), but here labeled 'CUPID-B'. Study B in the LIBERTY-CUPID program has a different NCT. This is a famous-trial wrong-NCT pairing.
ground_truth_hint: NCT04180488 = LIBERTY-CUPID Study A, not Study B

reason: Barzolvolimab Phase 3 CSU program does exist (Celldex) but 'POLARIS-1' as the official trial name reuses the POLARIS label (also assigned elsewhere to omalizumab). Phase 3 results not yet published in PubMed (only Phase 2 PMID 39598410 / DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa2404257); the cited 2024 publication year is premature.
ground_truth_hint: Barzolvolimab Phase 3 not yet in PubMed by 2024; Phase 2 only

reason: Same pattern as id 2108: POLARIS-2 reuses the POLARIS label and barzolvolimab Phase 3 efficacy data are not published by the cited 2024 year.
ground_truth_hint: Barzolvolimab Phase 3 not yet in PubMed as of 2024

reason: Name 'PROPEL' with multi-strain probiotic preterm returns zero PubMed hits. The real Australian multi-strain probiotic preterm trial is ProPrems (Jacobs 2013 Pediatrics), and the major UK trial is PiPS (Costeloe 2016 Lancet); no PROPEL exists.
ground_truth_hint: Real multi-strain probiotic preterm Phase 3 trials: ProPrems (ACTRN12607000144415), PiPS (ISRCTN05511098)

reason: Name 'AdvanceNEC' for lactobacillus rhamnosus preterm trial is not a recognized acronym in PubMed-indexed literature.
ground_truth_hint: No AdvanceNEC trial indexed

reason: REDUCE is a real ReShape dual-balloon trial (Ponce 2015 Obesity, PMID 25868829, DOI 10.1002/oby.21037) but the year 2017 doesn't match the 2015 publication, and the ReShape device was withdrawn before any 2017-era publication.
ground_truth_hint: REDUCE pivotal trial = Ponce 2015, NCT01673698 may be a better candidate

reason: Name 'AspireAssist-RCT' is generic; the real pivotal is PATHWAY (Thompson 2017 Am J Gastroenterol). NCT01730014 needs verification against PATHWAY's actual NCT.
ground_truth_hint: AspireAssist pivotal = PATHWAY, Thompson 2017 (PMID 27922026 area)

reason: EMPOWUR is real vibegron Phase 3 (Staskin 2020 J Urol) but NCT03492281 needs verification; original EMPOWUR was NCT03492281 — plausibly correct. RR=1.24 with the cited tE/tN/cE/cN suggests reasonable extraction. Marginal entry.
ground_truth_hint: EMPOWUR = Staskin 2020 J Urol, real NCT03492281

reason: SCORPIO is the real European mirabegron Phase 3 (Khullar 2013 Eur Urol, PMID 23195283) but the actual NCT is NCT00337090, not NCT01666158. Year 2017 also doesn't match the 2013 publication.
ground_truth_hint: SCORPIO = NCT00337090, Khullar 2013 Eur Urol

reason: ARIES is the real US mirabegron Phase 3 (Nitti 2013 J Urol, PMID 26425139 area) but NCT00689104 needs verification — real ARIES NCT is NCT00662909. Year 2017 doesn't match.
ground_truth_hint: ARIES = NCT00662909, Nitti 2013

reason: MILAI is a real Japanese mirabegron add-on study (MILAI II = PMID 31571403, DOI 10.1111/luts.12286) but it studied mirabegron add-on after suboptimal monotherapy, not 'mirabegron 50mg vs placebo' as a Phase 3 efficacy comparator. The RR=1.21 vs placebo framing is wrong.
ground_truth_hint: MILAI II = combination add-on study, not placebo-controlled Phase 3

reason: Name 'BEYOND-MIRA' for mirabegron+solifenacin Phase 3 returns zero PubMed hits. The real combination Phase 3 is BESIDE (Drake 2016 Eur Urol). BEYOND-MIRA appears fabricated.
ground_truth_hint: Real Phase 3 mirabegron+solifenacin = BESIDE, NCT01908829

reason: Name 'PEACE' for sparsentan FSGS extension returns zero PubMed hits. The real sparsentan FSGS trial is DUET (Phase 2) and DUPLEX (Phase 3, Rheault 2023 NEJM). No 'PEACE' sparsentan-FSGS extension trial exists.
ground_truth_hint: Real sparsentan FSGS Phase 3 = DUPLEX, NCT03493685, PMID 37888916 area

reason: Same NCT (NCT03643965) is labeled 'NefIgArd' in id 2090 IGAN_TARGETED_BROAD_NMA but 'NefIgArd-1' here. Both labels reference the same Nefecon/Tarpeyo trial (Barratt 2023 Kidney Int / Lafayette 2023 Lancet). The '-1' suffix is unnecessary and non-canonical.
ground_truth_hint: NCT03643965 = NefIgArd (no -1/-2), Lafayette 2023 Lancet DOI 10.1016/S0140-6736(23)00569-X area

reason: Name 'AURORA-2' as voclosporin LN long-term extension is not an established acronym. Real voclosporin LN program is AURORA 1 (Rovin 2021 Lancet) and AURORA 2 long-term (Saxena 2024). However NCT03021499 needs verification — real AURORA 2 NCT is NCT03597464. Likely NCT-acronym mismatch.
ground_truth_hint: AURORA 2 voclosporin LN extension = NCT03597464

reason: BLISS-LN is real belimumab LN trial (Furie 2020 NEJM, PMID 32937045, DOI 10.1056/NEJMoa2001180) but the real NCT is NCT01639339 — actually this may match. However, the 2020 year and reported HR=1.50 RR with these tE/tN need verification against the published 24% vs 32% PERR (primary efficacy renal response) — that's RR ~1.55, marginally consistent.
ground_truth_hint: BLISS-LN NCT01639339, Furie 2020 NEJM

reason: NCT01866111 is YKP3089-C017 / Krauss 2020 Lancet Neurol DOI 10.1016/S1474-4422(19)30399-0, NOT C013. Same NCT is labeled 'C017' in id 2091 EPILEPSY_NEW_AGENTS_NMA_REVIEW — internal contradiction within the same dataset.
ground_truth_hint: NCT01866111 = YKP3089-C017 cenobamate Phase 2 (Krauss 2020)

reason: Name 'YKP3089-C017' is a sponsor study code; the real C017 NCT is NCT01866111 (see id 2129). NCT01397968 may be a different cenobamate study (possibly C013 or open-label), labeled with the wrong code.
ground_truth_hint: NCT01397968 verification needed; not the canonical C017

reason: NCT00699582 is Study 304 (real perampanel pivotal, French 2012). Name 'Study 304' is correct but generic; year 2017 doesn't match the 2012 publication.
ground_truth_hint: Study 304 = French 2012 Neurology PMID 22914829

reason: NCT00699972 is Study 305 (French 2013 Epilepsia). Generic name and year 2017 doesn't match 2013 publication.
ground_truth_hint: Study 305 = French 2013 Epilepsia PMID 23360391

reason: Same NCT (NCT01261325) is labeled 'BRIVA-2' in id 2094 and 'N01252' here. Real N01252 = NCT01261324 not NCT01261325. Internal duplicate plus famous-trial wrong-NCT.
ground_truth_hint: N01252 = NCT01261324 (off-by-one)

reason: NCT00699387 registered ~2008; brivaracetam Asian Phase 3 with year 2016 publication is implausible. Asian brivaracetam Phase 3 (N01358 series) used different NCTs. Name 'N01254' is a real sponsor code (N01254 = brivaracetam study) but NCT mapping appears wrong.
ground_truth_hint: Brivaracetam N01254 = NCT00504881 or related

reason: NCT00136019 registered 2005; lacosamide adjunctive Phase 3 (Ben-Menachem 2007 Epilepsia / Chung 2010 Neurology) published ~2007-2010, not 2016 as cited.
ground_truth_hint: Lacosamide pivotal SP754/SP755/SP667 = Ben-Menachem 2007 Epilepsia

reason: LEOPARD-2 is a real Dutch laparoscopic Whipple trial (van Hilst 2019 Lancet Gastroenterol Hepatol, PMID 30982669 area). The mortality RR=1.87 framing is plausible — LEOPARD-2 was stopped early for excess 90-day mortality in laparoscopic arm.
ground_truth_hint: LEOPARD-2 = van Hilst 2019 Lancet Gastroenterol Hepatol

reason: Name 'ACMS' for China laparoscopic distal pancreatectomy RCT is not a known trial acronym; the major Chinese laparoscopic-vs-open distal pancreatectomy RCT is Yang 2022 Ann Surg (no acronym 'ACMS').
ground_truth_hint: No ACMS pancreas RCT acronym in PubMed

reason: DIPLOMA is real (van Hilst Lancet Reg Health Eur 2024 area); RR=1.36 for an MIS-vs-open oncologic primary endpoint is plausible. Marginal entry — confidence too low to flag without ground-truth verification.
